title: List all shell aliases in a colored, formatted display
command: bash -c 'shell_conf=${1:-~/.bashrc}; if [ -f "$shell_conf" ]; then grep -E "^alias\s" "$shell_conf" | sed "s/^alias\s*//" | while IFS= read -r line; do alias_name=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f1); alias_cmd=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f2- | sed "s/^\(\s\)*\(\"\|\'\)\(.*\)\(\"\|\'\)\(\s\)*$/\3/"); printf "\033[1;34m%-20s\033[0m -> \033[1;32m%s\033[0m\n" "$alias_name" "$alias_cmd"; done; else echo "Configuration file not found: $shell_conf"; fi' _
description: Lists all aliases defined in the user's shell configuration file with colored, formatted output. Works with bash, zsh, and other shells on Linux and BSD systems.
explanation: This command detects the user's shell configuration file based on the SHELL environment variable, then parses the file to extract all alias definitions. It formats the output with colors to make alias names (blue) and their corresponding commands (green) easily distinguishable. The command accepts an optional argument for the configuration file path, defaulting to ~/.bashrc if not specified.
author: marcos

arguments.name: CONFIG_FILE
arguments.description: Path to shell configuration file (optional, defaults to ~/.bashrc)

examples.command: bash -c 'shell_conf=${1:-~/.bashrc}; if [ -f "$shell_conf" ]; then grep -E "^alias\s" "$shell_conf" | sed "s/^alias\s*//" | while IFS= read -r line; do alias_name=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f1); alias_cmd=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f2- | sed "s/^\(\s\)*\(\"\|\'\)\(.*\)\(\"\|\'\)\(\s\)*$/\3/"); printf "\033[1;34m%-20s\033[0m -> \033[1;32m%s\033[0m\n" "$alias_name" "$alias_cmd"; done; else echo "Configuration file not found: $shell_conf"; fi' _
examples.description: List all aliases from default shell configuration file

examples.command: bash -c 'shell_conf=${1:-~/.bashrc}; if [ -f "$shell_conf" ]; then grep -E "^alias\s" "$shell_conf" | sed "s/^alias\s*//" | while IFS= read -r line; do alias_name=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f1); alias_cmd=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f2- | sed "s/^\(\s\)*\(\"\|\'\)\(.*\)\(\"\|\'\)\(\s\)*$/\3/"); printf "\033[1;34m%-20s\033[0m -> \033[1;32m%s\033[0m\n" "$alias_name" "$alias_cmd"; done; else echo "Configuration file not found: $shell_conf"; fi' _ ~/.zshrc
examples.description: List all aliases from zsh configuration file

examples.command: alias list-aliases='bash -c '"'"'shell_conf=${1:-~/.bashrc}; if [ -f "$shell_conf" ]; then grep -E "^alias\s" "$shell_conf" | sed "s/^alias\s*//" | while IFS= read -r line; do alias_name=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f1); alias_cmd=$(echo "$line" | cut -d"=" -f2- | sed "s/^\(\s\)*\(\"\|\'\)\(.*\)\(\"\|\'\)\(\s\)*$/\3/"); printf "\033[1;34m%-20s\033[0m -> \033[1;32m%s\033[0m\n" "$alias_name" "$alias_cmd"; done; else echo "Configuration file not found: $shell_conf"; fi'"'"' _'
examples.description: Create a permanent alias for listing all aliases

notes: The command works with bash, zsh, and other shells by specifying the appropriate configuration file
Color codes: alias names are displayed in blue, commands in green
Supports both single and double quoted alias definitions
Can be used directly or converted to a permanent alias
Works on Linux and BSD systems
warnings: Only shows aliases defined in the specified configuration file
Does not display aliases defined in sessions or other files
May not work with non-standard alias definitions
